next_best_action: Investigate comms relay compatibility with the latest station firmware update. Check for known RF mesh antenna issues on Deck 4. Verify SubComm v3.2 is the latest supported version.
remediation_steps: Verify SubComm v3.2 is the latest supported version for the station firmware
Check for known RF mesh antenna connectivity issues on Deck 4
Test comms relay handoff behavior between hardline and RF mesh after the firmware update on a reference terminal
If issue persists, escalate to communications engineering for firmware patch review

next_best_action: Investigate whether Mission Planner crash and Star Chart waypoint sync issues are related to the terminal swap. Verify that crew profile and application data were properly migrated to the new console.
remediation_steps: Verify terminal provisioning and data migration completed successfully
Clear Mission Planner cache and reinstall if crash persists
Check Star Chart profile migration status and force waypoint database resync
Confirm software versions match the supported standard station image
If issues persist after cache clear and resync, re-provision applications on the new terminal

next_best_action: Dispatch immediate on-site support to the main briefing chamber with a portable backup holo-emitter. Time-critical: $2.3B colony contract review with Deep Space Command delegation in 25 minutes.
remediation_steps: Dispatch on-site technician to the main briefing chamber immediately
Bring portable backup holo-emitter from engineering supply
Check projector power source, data conduit connections, and input source settings
If main holographic projector cannot be restored, set up portable unit before the delegation arrives
After briefing, schedule full diagnostic of the main chamber holographic projector

next_best_action: Contact crew member to gather specifics: which subsystems are inaccessible, what error messages appear, what console they are using, and when the issue started. Cannot triage effectively without more detail.
remediation_steps: Contact crew member to gather specific details about the issue
Determine which subsystems and applications are affected
Check if the issue is isolated to this crew member or affecting others in the Sensors division
Once root cause is identified, route to the appropriate specialist team

next_best_action: Close — this is an automated shore leave auto-reply to a maintenance notification, not a mission signal. Reporter is at Titan Station through March 28. No action required.
remediation_steps: No action required -- auto-reply detected, close signal

next_best_action: Initiate security incident investigation. Review biometric authentication logs for unauthorized access, check data vault audit trail for document access from the unregistered terminal, and verify that access codes have been reset and multi-factor authentication is in effect.
remediation_steps: Review biometric authentication logs for the crew member's profile over the past 48 hours
Check data vault audit trail for any unauthorized file access or downloads
Verify access code reset was successful and multi-factor biometric authentication is active
If unauthorized access is confirmed, escalate to threat response and notify the station data protection officer
Brief crew member on interim security measures and monitor profile for further anomalies

next_best_action: Check service credentials and role assignments on the station data archive in data-core-prod. Restore the Data Contributor role if it was removed during the archive cleanup.
remediation_steps: Verify service credential expiration for the telemetry pipeline identity
Check role assignments on the station data archive in data-core-prod
Restore Data Contributor role assignment if missing
Re-run the telemetry pipeline 'prod-sensor-ingest' and verify successful execution
Investigate why the role assignment was removed during the archive cleanup to prevent recurrence

next_best_action: Investigate production telemetry database connection pool exhaustion immediately. This is a station-critical incident despite the reporter's 'low priority' label — 40% of sensor queries are failing and life support monitoring data has gone stale. The oxygen recycler dashboard hasn't updated in 45 minutes. Override priority to P1.
remediation_steps: Investigate connection pool configuration on the primary telemetry data node immediately
Identify and terminate long-running queries or leaked connections causing pool exhaustion
Verify life support monitoring systems are receiving data — oxygen recycler dashboard gap is the top concern
Increase connection pool size or restart the primary node as immediate mitigation
Confirm backup node health and verify it is handling redirected traffic
Post-incident: conduct root cause analysis on connection pool exhaustion and add alerting on pool usage thresholds

next_best_action: Escalate airlock biometric badge issue for Deck 7 — third report without resolution. Crew member has been manually overriding the door daily since the access system migration. Verify badge permissions in the updated access management system and cross-reference with SIG-2834.
remediation_steps: Check crew member's badge permissions in the updated access management system
Verify Deck 7 airlock access rights were properly migrated from the old system
If permissions appear correct, test the physical badge and biometric scanner at the airlock
Escalate to station facilities management if badge and permissions are both confirmed working
Follow up with crew member to confirm resolution

next_best_action: Initiate new crew member onboarding workflow for Alex Rivera (arriving 0600 supply shuttle, March 31). Coordinate console terminal, software, access, and quarters provisioning across teams. Target readiness by March 28 for orientation walkthrough.
remediation_steps: Requisition high-spec console terminal with 32GB memory core from equipment stores
Prepare standard flight software image with SciAnalytics and SpectraLab suite
Request telemetry workspace access (lab-science-prod) from data operations
Request data archive read access from telemetry engineering
Request Deck 3 quarters assignment (avoid atmospheric processor-adjacent quarters) and lab badge from crew services
Add Alex Rivera to science-team comm channel and briefing distribution list
Schedule equipment handoff and orientation walkthrough for March 28

next_best_action: Investigate iris scanner biometric match failure. Crew member returned from a 6-hour EVA recently — check whether BioAuth registration needs recalibration after prolonged EVA exposure. Backup PIN code works as interim access method.
remediation_steps: Verify biometric registration status for this crew member in the BioAuth system
Check whether prolonged EVA exposure or lighting adjustment can affect iris scan calibration
Check for recent authentication or biometric policy changes that may affect iris scanning
If issue persists, remove and re-register the iris biometric pattern in BioAuth
Confirm iris scan works after re-registration and crew member no longer needs the backup PIN

next_best_action: Investigate sensor array display flickering as primary issue -- likely hardware or data conduit fault. Route the NavStar Pro license question separately to Mission Software Operations.
remediation_steps: Test with a replacement data conduit cable to rule out cable fault
Test display with a different source terminal to isolate whether it's the display or the console
Check for electromagnetic interference from nearby station systems that could cause intermittent flickering
If flickering persists with new cable and source, schedule display replacement
Create a separate signal for the NavStar Pro license expiry and route to Mission Software Operations

next_best_action: Initiate data breach investigation immediately. This is a potential regulatory incident involving classified PII exposure of 1,200 crew personnel under Space Authority data protection regulations. Determine scope of unauthorized access, identify who created the sharing link, and assess notification obligations.
remediation_steps: Confirm the unsecured sharing permissions have been fully revoked and verify no other archives have similar settings
Pull data archive audit logs to identify who created the public sharing link and when
Analyze audit logs for any unauthorized access to the archive between February 15 and today
Engage Tactical Operations and Legal to assess Space Authority notification obligations
Document findings for the incident report and regulatory record
Conduct a broader data archive permissions audit across all archives containing classified crew PII

next_best_action: Close — this is a thank-you message about a resolved atmospheric recycler issue, not a new mission signal. No action required.
remediation_steps: No action required -- positive feedback, close signal

next_best_action: Investigate 200ms to 50ms latency regression on the Gamma Sector navigation relay before the Centauri transit window. Check relay path to the navigation beacon, recent routing or signal barrier changes, and whether the latency is on the station's side or the beacon side.
remediation_steps: Check navigation relay latency metrics on the path between the station's Gamma Sector relay and the navigation beacon
Review recent signal barrier, routing, or relay configuration changes that may affect the navigation path
Compare current latency baseline against historical data to confirm the regression
If latency is on the station's side, escalate to communications engineering for immediate remediation before the Centauri transit window
If beacon-side, contact beacon operations and notify helm officers of potential impact

next_best_action: Investigate SSO token invalidation after credential rotation. Likely the FlightOS authentication ticket needs to be reissued after the station credential change. Time-sensitive due to end-of-cycle trajectory calculation deadline.
remediation_steps: Verify the crew member's station session and authentication ticket are refreshed after the credential rotation
Check FlightOS SSO configuration -- confirm whether FlightOS uses token-based or certificate-based SSO
Reissue the FlightOS SSO logon ticket or have the crew member log into FlightOS with explicit credentials as a workaround
If SSO token regeneration doesn't resolve it, check whether the rotation protocol affected the FlightOS secure channel configuration
Confirm FlightOS access is restored and the crew member can complete end-of-cycle trajectory calculations

next_best_action: Contact reporter on Deck 5 (Robert Chen, robert.chen2@cdss.space, Engineering) to gather actual symptom details. Signal as received has insufficient detail to triage. Emergency beacon transcription is too garbled to act on.
remediation_steps: Contact Robert Chen (Deck 5 near the galley, Engineering division) to clarify the issue in person or via holodeck comm
Determine which application or subsystem is failing
Gather specific anomaly readouts and reproduction steps
Once the actual issue is identified, re-triage and route to the correct specialist team

next_best_action: Dispatch support to the command deck briefing chamber immediately. Commander-level VIP signal -- treat as one priority level higher. Investigate holographic display sharing policy blocking projection for this crew member. Active command briefing with admiralty.
remediation_steps: Dispatch technician to the command deck briefing chamber immediately
Check station access policies and holographic display permissions applied to Commander Martinez's account
Verify whether the 'Display sharing blocked by station policy' error is caused by a security policy, display admin policy, or access restriction
As immediate workaround: have someone else project the presentation on his behalf, or use the chamber's integrated holo-emitter with a different account
Once the briefing is unblocked, investigate and resolve the underlying policy conflict

next_best_action: Initiate crew member departure workflow for Jessica Palmer (transferring to Kepler Station, last duty day March 21, 1800 shuttle). Coordinate across Crew Identity, Spacecraft Systems Engineering, and Telemetry & Data Core. Note: classified Sector 7-G investigation files require special handling.
remediation_steps: Schedule BioScan ID disable for 18:00 EST on March 21 — not before, as crew member needs airlock access to reach shuttle bay
Prepare airlock access and remote terminal privilege revocation to trigger simultaneously with BioScan disable
Coordinate with Spacecraft Systems Engineering for terminal wipe of StarTerm X-40 (asset tag SS-T-4892)
Transfer data vault file ownership to David Liu (david.liu@cdss.space)
Remove from all comm channels and briefing distribution lists
Archive message logs per 7-year retention protocol
Ensure classified Sector 7-G investigation files are preserved in a secured location and access is revoked

next_best_action: Investigate fabricator output queue issue on Deck 3. Multiple crew affected and jobs are queuing but nothing materializes. Replacement hull gaskets for the observation deck viewport are needed within 72 hours — this needs resolution before a P3 becomes a P1.
remediation_steps: Identify the fabricator model and check whether it is network-attached or connected via a fabrication controller
Check fabrication controller spooler service status and restart if necessary
Verify network connectivity between the fabricator and the controller or direct data path
If network-connected, check the fabricator's network address and interface status
Clear stuck fabrication queue and test with a fresh job submission
Prioritize hull gasket fabrication jobs once the fabricator is operational

next_best_action: Redirect to Station Operations — briefing chamber reservations and catering are not Mission Control functions. Note: the Mission Justification Code is only required for official mission briefings, not crew socials.
remediation_steps: Reply with instructions: for a crew social, select 'Social Event' as the booking type in the station scheduling system — this bypasses the Mission Justification Code requirement
Note that catering requests should go to the Station Operations team on Deck 6, not Mission Control
If the crew member cannot see chamber resources in the scheduling system, verify their profile has the chamber booking permission and route to Mission Software Operations if needed

next_best_action: Renew or replace the encryption certificate on relay-gateway.cdss.space before it expires on March 20. This is the primary external comms relay gateway -- expiry will break all external communications and allied fleet integrations. Treat as P1 given the 3-day window and the fact that the original engineer has transferred off-station.
remediation_steps: Identify the current certificate authority and certificate type (public CA, internal PKI, or auto-provisioned)
Determine whether auto-renewal was configured or if the cert was manually provisioned
If auto-renewal exists, trigger a manual renewal cycle and verify the new cert is issued
If manual, generate a certificate signing request, obtain a new certificate from the CA, and install it on the relay gateway
Verify the new certificate is correctly installed and all external comms links work without encryption errors
Configure auto-renewal and monitoring alerts to prevent recurrence

next_best_action: Investigate terminal performance degradation. Symptoms (slow performance, fan running, pop-ups about 'updating system components') are more consistent with a stuck system update or background compliance scan than malware, especially since the onboard security scanner found nothing. Check update history and system resource usage.
remediation_steps: Check system update history -- verify whether recent patches completed or are stuck retrying
Review system monitor for high CPU/disk processes (likely system update or compliance scan)
If updates are stuck, run system update troubleshooter or manually reset the update components
Verify security scanner definitions are current and run a diagnostic scan to confirm no threats
If the crew member opened a suspicious transmission, forward the transmission to security@cdss.space and notify Threat Response Command as a precaution

next_best_action: Confirm this is a phishing/scam transmission and advise the crew member NOT to access any links. Forward the original transmission to security@cdss.space for analysis. No breach has occurred if the crew member hasn't clicked.
remediation_steps: Reply to the crew member immediately: this is a scam transmission -- do NOT access any links or provide any personal information
Have the crew member forward the original transmission as an attachment to security@cdss.space
Check whether the transmission bypassed the station's comm filtering and report to Threat Response Command if so
If the crew member already accessed the link, escalate to a full security incident and initiate credential reset

next_best_action: Investigate compute node prod-analytics-01 stuck at 'Applying configuration' after scheduled OS patching reboot. Command analytics dashboard depends on this node -- 2-hour window before the 9 AM mission briefing.
remediation_steps: Check boot diagnostics console output for the stuck configuration state
Attempt to connect via diagnostic serial console to bypass the stuck boot screen
If serial console access is possible, check if a startup policy or initialization script is hanging
If the node is unrecoverable, redeploy from the latest snapshot or restore from backup
Verify the command analytics dashboard is accessible once the node is restored
Post-incident: review the OS patching procedure to prevent boot failures after scheduled updates
